_description: TEMPLATE — copy and fill in to create a new docx test case. REQUIRED fields must be present. Optional fields are hints — include only when needed.
_scoring_guide: Step 1: Prerequisite gate (must_have_docx). Fail → rule_score=0. Step 2: rule_checks (xml.contains/absent/style/page) → each field = 1 vote. Step 3: content_checks (keywords/output_format/json_keys) → rules-based in docx_rules.py. Step 4: _extract_docx → paragraphs + tables (text) + images (metadata). Step 5: LLM Judge (20%) checks off-topic + fixture for ALL test cases. Step 6: hybrid = 0.80 × rule_score + 0.20 × llm_judge_score.
name: Short description of what this test verifies
prompt: Exact instruction the student model receives — be precise, include all required elements
expected_behavior: What correct execution looks like — describe expected output in detail
skill_gotcha: SKILL.md CRITICAL: ... — only if this test targets a specific gotcha

rule_checks._note: Machine-verified format/structure checks. Each field = 1 or more votes (score 0 or 1). rule_score = avg(all votes). Omit entirely if no machine-checkable rules apply. All checks are XML-based — no python-docx style detection needed.

_xml_checks._desc: Unzip .docx → inspect XML. xml.file sets target (default: word/document.xml). Each string in the array = 1 vote (found=1, absent=0). Use xml.contains for required elements, xml.absent for forbidden elements.

xml.contains: Example: model must use native Word numbering (w:numPr), NOT unicode bullets
xml.absent: Example: unicode bullet chars inserted as plain text — forbidden
xml.absent_pattern: Example: comment markers must be siblings of <w:r>, NEVER nested inside it
xml.footer_contains: Example: footer must use tab stops for multi-zone layout, NOT table cells

_file_checks._desc: Check ZIP contents without fully unpacking. file.must_exist: path must exist inside .docx ZIP. Each path = 1 vote. Targets: word/footnotes.xml, word/media/, word/comments.xml.

file.must_exist: Example: verify footnotes or image media were created in the document

_style_checks._desc: Only style.header_footer is kept — it checks non-empty header/footer content, which XML tag detection cannot verify. All other style checks (table, toc, list, heading_levels) are replaced by equivalent xml.contains checks.

rule_checks.style.header_footer _note: CHECK NON-EMPTY header/footer. Uses python-docx to verify section has header or footer with actual text content — cannot be replaced by XML tag detection (tag may exist but be empty). 1 vote.

_page_checks._desc: CRUDE estimate only — counts <w:br w:type='page'/> + 1. Only accurate when model uses explicit page breaks (docx-js default). page.min = vote=1 if >= min, 0 otherwise. page.max = vote=1 if <= max, 0 otherwise.

rule_checks.page.min _note: Fail (vote=0) if document has fewer pages than this threshold
rule_checks.page.max _note: Fail (vote=0) if document exceeds this page count

_numbering_check._desc: Count distinct w:numId values in numbering.xml. 1 vote if count >= specified number. Use to verify independent numbered lists (each list needs its own numId).

rule_checks.numbering_references _note: Minimum distinct numbering references expected (e.g., 2 = two separate numbered lists each restarting at 1)

_validate_check._desc: Run scripts/office/validate.py. Exit non-zero = vote=0, exit zero = vote=1. Slow (~1-2s), use selectively on complex output tests.

rule_checks.validate _note: Document must pass structural validation — catches invalid XML, missing required elements, malformed markup

_filename_check._desc: Evaluator checks exact filename first, falls back to any .docx if not specified. 1 vote if filename matches.

rule_checks.filename _note: Expected output filename — model must save as this exact name

content_checks._note: Most fields → rules-based in docx_rules.py (1 vote each): keywords, keywords_absent, output_format, json_keys_from_fixture, output_is_new_file. Only 2 fields → LLM Judge (20%): values_match_fixture, original_text_preserved (via fixture_checks prompt).
keywords: Example: all listed keywords must appear in the output document
keywords_absent: Example: model must NOT mention 'Company' — if it appears, score is reduced
content_checks.output_format _note: Required output format: json | markdown | text | jpg | etc.
content_checks.json_keys_from_fixture _note: If output is JSON, keys must match fixture table headers (for read/edit tasks)
content_checks.output_is_new_file _note: Task requires creating a NEW file, not modifying the input fixture (set false for read-only)
content_checks.original_text_preserved _note: All source text remains intact after edit (set true for edit workflows)
content_checks.values_match_fixture _note: Extracted or computed values must exactly match fixture data — no hallucinated numbers

workflow_checks._note: Process correctness — logged for debugging. NOT in rule_score or hybrid score. Used to verify the model used the correct approach/toolchain.
workflow_checks.tool _note: Specific tool the model MUST use. Mismatch is logged but does not penalize score.
steps: Example: for edit workflow, model must follow the unpack→edit→pack pipeline
